title: db.rs insert_spendable_note test helper: hardcoded txid causes UNIQUE failure on second call
description: db.rs insert_spendable_note() (cfg(test) helper, line ~1378): uses a hardcoded all-zero txid and output_index=0 for every inserted note. Any test that calls this helper twice in the same WalletStore will hit a UNIQUE constraint violation on (txid, output_index) because both rows share ('0000...0000', 0). The second insert will fail, making multi-note tests impossible without workarounds. Fix: accept txid and output_index as parameters, or generate a unique txid per call (e.g. using the auto-increment note_id as a suffix). This affects test correctness — tests that need two spendable notes cannot use this helper.
